commit.message:
Fix bot 409 conflict: retry with backoff instead of crashing

When Telegram returns 409 (another getUpdates request), wait
5-30s and retry instead of crashing the process, which caused
Docker to instantly restart and repeat the conflict loop.

commit.message:
Law of Zero Identity: purge TAG_ORDER, sort tags alphabetically

- io/mod.rs: delete TAG_ORDER (biological column order has no place in the Stone)
- io/json.rs: remove FIELD_ORDER; fixed fields + alphabetical tags only
- io/parquet_regions.rs: schema discovery sorts tag columns alphabetically;
  update test assertions to reflect pure alphabetical ordering (no biological preference)
- models/preset.rs: add distance_label: Option<String> to FeatureDefinition
- presets/tiling/saccer3_features.yaml: add distance_label per feature
- pipeline/design_crispr_library.rs: read distance_label from YAML at runtime,
  inject dynamically; drop total_hits (= off_targets+1, redundant) and .with_score

The Stone collects tags, sorts them algorithmically, and passes to Sink.
No hardcoded biological name survives in compiled Rust.

feat: KNOX tiered kernel scan v2.0 — structured 3-tier diagnostics

Restructure Agent KNOX from flat alert list into formal kernel scan:

TIER 1 ◈ VITAL INTEGRITY
  - Priority 01: Health Shield (Medical budget status)
  - Priority 02: Essential Coverage

TIER 2 ◈ STRUCTURAL INTEGRITY
  - Priority 03: Fortress Wall Phase 1 ($1K target)
  - NL-1: Discretionary > 2% income warning + ACTION redirect
  - NL-4: Blown category alerts
  - Interest burn rate
  - E-Fund runway

TIER 3 ◈ LIBERATION PROGRESS
  - NL-2: Minimum-only on high APR debt
  - NL-3: Active positions in Defense Mode
  - Priority 04: Debt Liberation
  - Financial Velocity scan

Tags: [DANGER]/[WARNING]/[VERIFIED]/[SCANNING]/[ACTION]
Each alert shows ↳ REC: recommendation line
Dark mode detection via hex red-channel parsing (reliable)
Footer: KNOX v2.0 | N CRITICAL | Stage X / 7

commit.message:
Fix chat dock dragging with scroll on iOS

- Removed visualViewport scroll listener — only resize (keyboard open/close)
  is needed. The scroll listener caused the dock to jitter when scrolling messages.
- Removed transform:translateY(offsetTop) — not needed with position:fixed,
  which anchors to the layout viewport and doesn't move when iOS scrolls.
- Added body overflow:hidden in chat mode to prevent page-level bouncing.
- Added overscroll-behavior:contain on scroll area to prevent scroll chaining.
- Added touch-action:manipulation on dock to prevent double-tap zoom.

commit.message:
feat: Task 1.4 — CoW snapshot engine with page pool, dirty list, and EPT fault handler (#10)

Implements the full Copy-on-Write snapshot engine for phantom.ko:

- ept_cow.c/h: pre-allocated NUMA-local page pool (lock-free LIFO),
  EPT fault handler (phantom_cow_fault, hot-path clean), dirty list
  tracking, and snapshot restore (phantom_cow_abort_iteration with
  batched single-context INVEPT per Intel SDM §28.3.3.1)

- memory.c/h: atomic64 memory accounting enforcing phantom_max_memory_mb
  soft limit, checked before pool init

- ept.c/h: phantom_ept_mark_all_ro marks all RAM PTEs read-only at
  snapshot time (write-protect without clearing read/exec)

- vmx_core.h/c: added CoW state fields after host_rsp (preserving asm
  trampoline offsets); wires pool init, abort_iteration, and dirty list
  into vmcs_setup/teardown and the vCPU thread loop

- debug.c/h: phantom_debug_dump_dirty_list emits DIRTY_ENTRY lines
  via trace_printk using last_dirty_count (saved before abort resets
  dirty_count to 0)

- interface.c/h: version bumped to 0x00010400; added three guest
  binaries (test_id=2 CoW 20-page write, test_id=3 pool exhaustion,
  test_id=4 absent-GPA write rejection); added DUMP_DIRTY_LIST ioctl

- Fix: LIFO pool off-by-one — head initialised to capacity (not
  capacity-1) so alloc returns pages[capacity-1] on first call and
  free writes to pages[idx-1] not pages[idx], eliminating the
  double-free that caused BUG: Bad page state on pool destroy

- tests/test_1_4_cow.c: 14 ioctl assertions covering all test scenarios
- tests/test_1_4_cow.sh: 11-check shell harness with SIGPIPE-safe grep
  patterns and dmesg clear at start

All 14 ioctl tests and 11 shell tests pass in the QEMU guest.

Co-authored-by: ChromeOS Developer <developer@localhost>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: tela de dados complementares + controle de acesso por CPF (v1.2)

Backend (AS - todos os ambientes):
- Remove sistema de vouchers (RVK-XXXXX) completamente
- Adiciona complementarCadastro(): valida unicidade do CPF antes de liberar
- validarToken() retorna step: 'complementar' | 'teste' para o frontend
- Cadastros ganha colunas: CPF, DataNasc, Gênero, CEP, Logradouro, Número,
  Complemento, Bairro, Cidade, Estado (preenchidas em etapa separada)
- Remove scope script.scriptapp do appsscript.json

Frontend:
- Nova tela comp-screen (Dados Complementares) entre token e teste
- Campos: Nome (readonly), CPF, Data Nascimento, Gênero, CEP + busca ViaCEP,
  Logradouro, Número, Complemento, Bairro, Cidade, Estado
- mascaraCPF() e mascaraCEP() com formatação automática
- buscarCEP() via ViaCEP para preenchimento automático do endereço
- salvarComplemento() valida e envia ao AS, depois inicia o teste
- Fluxo: cover → postoken → token → comp-screen → teste → resultados
- Token com status 'Complementado' vai direto ao teste (step === 'teste')

commit.message:
Add geo-mask pruning, wide 128-bit solver, and parameter validation

- compute_geo_masks: prune immovable block configurations (edge-adjacent
  pairs, 2x2 squares) before bitmask enumeration to skip dead configs
- solve_hash128: 128-bit BFS solver for large block+hole counts (>64 bits)
  dispatched automatically when 5+5*nb+nh exceeds uint64_t capacity
- MAX_BLOCKS 8→24, MAX_HOLES 13→23
- --nholes validated against 23 - num_blocks; default nh_hi capped at
  min(total-nw, 23-total)

Move v1/elements/sessions code from payments-core to paymentsheet (#12502)

* Move v1/elements/sessions code from payments-core to paymentsheet

The elements session API call and related models only exist for
paymentsheet consumers. Moving them out of payments-core reduces the
StripeRepository surface and co-locates the code with its callers.

- Move ElementsSession, ElementsSessionParams, DeferredIntentParams
  models and their parsers to the paymentsheet module
- Replace StripeRepository.retrieveElementsSession() with a direct
  StripeNetworkClient call in RealElementsSessionRepository
- Split GetFinancialConnectionsAvailability: the ElementsSession-aware
  version moves to paymentsheet, a simpler default helper stays in
  payments-core for CollectBankAccountLauncher
- Update DI modules to include StripeNetworkClientModule
- Move associated test files and update test infrastructure

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Committed-By-Agent: claude

the fire-and-forget fraud detection data refresh that warms the cache for

(following the LinkApiRepository pattern) and calling refresh() at the start
of get().

commit.message:
Fix invisible stepper: remove reveal animation from header and stepper

The header and stepper had opacity:0 via cs-transform__reveal class,
relying on IntersectionObserver to make them visible. The observer
can fail in Shopify theme editor iframes or when elements are already
in viewport on load, leaving the stepper permanently hidden.

Fix: remove reveal class from header and stepper (always visible).
Add 3s fallback animation for remaining reveal elements.

Directory Opus 5 for AmigaOS 4 — initial commit

Source code from the DOpus5 All Amigas SourceForge project
(https://sourceforge.net/projects/dopus5allamigas/) with fixes
for building and running on AmigaOS 4.1.

Fixes applied:
- Load Environment deadlock (IPC_ListCommand must be async)
- Environment process parent/child exit error (sync OPEN_DISPLAY)
- Screen close abort button (escape infinite retry loop)
- NULL backdrop guard in close_display
- DOPUS5: and D5THEMES: assign leaks on exit
- MEMF_PRIVATE fallback for zero memory type
- ModuleInfo flexible array sizing in 6 modules
- BltBitMapTags modernisation (6 call sites)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add MarkdownField and TiptapRichTextField editor components (#90)

* Add MarkdownField and TiptapRichTextField to sandbox

Add two new rich-text editor components to the app-sandbox, copying
the implementation from browser-app design-system input components
but exposing a simplified public API consistent with TextField.

- MarkdownField: Wraps the OverType markdown editor with formatting
  toolbar, label/description/layout support, and string|null value API
- TiptapRichTextField: Wraps the Tiptap rich-text editor with full
  formatting toolbar, label/description/layout support, and
  JSONContent|null value API
- Update index.d.ts with public API documentation for both components
- Register both components in sandbox dependencies

https://claude.ai/code/session_01LAMynWopQsXCmHieXuqWe7

* Restructure MarkdownField and TiptapRichTextField to use Eager pattern

Follow the same lazy-loading pattern as Echart: thin wrapper component
with Suspense + Skeleton fallback, separate Eager* file with all logic,
and shared Props.ts type.

- MarkdownField.tsx → thin lazy wrapper with Skeleton fallback
- EagerMarkdownField.tsx → merged field structure + OverType editor
- TiptapRichTextField.tsx → thin lazy wrapper with Skeleton fallback
- EagerTiptapRichTextField.tsx → merged field structure + Tiptap editor
- Remove MarkdownInput.tsx and TiptapInput.tsx (merged into Eager files)
- Extract Props.ts for each component

https://claude.ai/code/session_01LAMynWopQsXCmHieXuqWe7

* Deploy sandbox alongside demo app for PR previews

Previously, PR preview deployments pointed to the production sandbox
at app-sandbox.demo.superego.dev, which doesn't reflect PR changes.

Now the preview build includes app-sandbox.html as a Vite entry point
and sets VITE_SANDBOX_URL=/app-sandbox.html so the demo app loads the
sandbox from the same Cloudflare Pages deployment.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01LAMynWopQsXCmHieXuqWe7

* Simplify MarkdownField/TiptapRichTextField APIs

- Remove layout prop from both fields (always use vertical)
- TiptapRichTextField now outputs Superego JsonObject values
  (branded with __dataType: DataType.JsonObject), matching how
  browser-app's TiptapRichText format works
- Remove JSONContent interface from index.d.ts, use any with
  documentation that the value must be a valid Tiptap JSONContent
  object branded as a Superego JsonObject

https://claude.ai/code/session_01LAMynWopQsXCmHieXuqWe7

* fix: fixes to vibed code, small TiptapInput refactor

* fix: address PR comments

commit.message:
Fix lease JE: deletion order, duplicate display, and partial generation

Three critical fixes:
1. DELETE handler: delete lease first (cascades schedule FK refs),
   then delete orphaned JEs — fixes FK violation preventing JE cleanup
2. Transactions page: add lease schedule JE IDs to coveredJeIds set
   so JE lines don't appear as duplicates alongside schedule rows
3. JE generation: await instead of fire-and-forget (prevents serverless
   timeout killing partial generation), parallel batch of 10 for speed
4. PATCH terminate: null out schedule FK refs before deleting future JEs
